channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says US not willing to make deal with China unless trade deficit is solved
item.description: President Trump said on Sunday during a gaggle with reporters on Air Force One that he is only willing to make a trade deal with China if the deficit is solved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Colorado portrait artist says career in jeopardy
item.description: The artist behind a portrait of President Trump that hung at the Colorado Capitol for six years says the president's comments are "negatively impacting" her business.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Left-wing movie director Oliver Stone rips Democrats’ ‘lying’ Russiagate probe against Trump
item.description: Left-wing movie director Oliver Stone slammed Democrats for weaponizing federal law enforcement against Trump in 2016, and applauded the president for taking steps to go after those involved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For these seniors, DOGE's affordable housing pause means suffering longer without AC
item.description: The Trump administration froze funding for a program to upgrade aging low-income housing and make it energy efficient. The move threatens hundreds of projects around the country.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden, MPR News

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu will meet again Monday on Gaza, tariffs and more
item.description: Netanyahu's office in a statement on social media said he and Trump would discuss “the tariff issue, the efforts to return our hostages.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge awards $6.6 million to whistleblowers who reported Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton to FBI
item.description: A district court judge on Friday awarded more than $6 million combined to four whistleblowers in their lawsuit against Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton who were fired shortly after they reported him to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House Monday
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson moves on Senate's Trump budget bill as House GOP rebels threaten to defect
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson is rallying House Republicans around the reconciliation process after the Senate passed its own version of such a bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate passes budget, setting up showdown with House over Trump agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans voted early Saturday morning to pass a budget resolution that will be critical to advancing President Trump’s legislative agenda, but the measure breaks with House Republicans on several big issues, setting the stage for a showdown between the two chambers later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-lawmaker George Santos faces 7-year prison sentence for federal fraud, identity theft
item.description: Disgraced ex-congressman George Santos could receive 7 years in prison after pleading guilty to federal fraud charges. Prosecutors allege he stole from donors and misused campaign funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In the race to save lives after the Myanmar quake, US rescuers are notable by their absence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Day after day, Chinese rescue teams haul children and elderly people from collapsed buildings as cameras beam the thanks of grateful survivors around the world. Russian medical teams show off field hospitals erected in a flash to tend the wounded.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet its leaders following recent U.S. allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker proposes additional Kratom regulations; advocates partially agree
item.description: State lawmakers are looking to add more regulations to the controversial herbal supplement known as Kratom.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration ends key grant program that helps communities prepare for disasters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Trump administration weighs the future of the federal agency tasked with responding to disasters, it is ending a key program that has been used by communities across the country to pay for projects designed to help them prepare for natural disasters like flooding and fires.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom pursues trade partnerships to shield California from tariff impact
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom's office announced on Friday plans to shield California's economy from the ripple effects of the Trump administration's escalating tariff war.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US must return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to an El Salvador prison, a judge says
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison, while a U.S. government attorney was at a loss to explain what happened.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet with leaders of the Central American country following recent Trump administration allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Energy Department identifies thousands of nonessential positions at risk of DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Energy Department has identified thousands of federal workers it deems "nonessential” and would not be protected if there is another round of large-scale firings, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trade war escalates as China hits back with 34% tariffs on all U.S. goods
item.description: Analysts say the escalating trade tensions between the U.S and China will make a near-term deal to end the trade war "highly unlikely".
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Fed chair: Tariffs could hike inflation, but more details needed
item.description: Powell's public appearance comes as Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs continue to rock economic markets worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to run as independent in mayoral election
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams, a Democrat, is planning to run as an independent in the 2025 mayoral election, he announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Fired or frustrated federal workers may have eager employers waiting for them in Minnesota
item.description: For federal workers, the past few months have been filled with uncertainty as the Trump administration pushes ahead with efforts to reduce the size of government agencies. But whether they are forced or choose to leave, those workers may find employers eager to hire them.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5
